module_mood_contract: expanded horizon — 'oh, we just did the thing we were worried about doing.' Forward awe with a deliverable in hand. Resolves to 'now you know what to do,' never 'we are done here.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per phase.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All teaching beats score 8+/10. Phase 1 (Opening) = 9; Phase 3 (What to Notice) = 8; Phase 4 (Close) = 8. Close uses 'carry forward' and 'build the same reflex' — forward-hungry register, not terminal completion. The stale trace's 'done' language (from a prior file version) is gone.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3 unease not preempted.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close: 'The next section turns that live pattern into a small system you run on your own.' Hands off cleanly to M3's capable-next-step mood without delivering M3's teaching moment. No 'you've now mastered the system' resolution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: What to Notice bullet 4 ('The room just used the same system shape: people add judgment; the folder structure keeps that judgment moving') fires post-exercise when the lived experience is fresh. The teaching moment is named in the body after the exercise, not before — correct placement. Persona at any competence level would have just completed the 60-minute exercise and the naming registers as recognition. Strategy doc's magic beat ('you protected signal before comparing answers') is an additional live organiser verbal move; the curriculum body's version is complementary, not duplicate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file leads with strategy doc's Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File Big Idea: 'The hard part isn't picking a tool. It's finding the challenge that matters.' Strategy doc: 'The hard part isn't picking a tool — it's finding the crux.' Minor word substitution (challenge vs crux) is acceptable — module body immediately uses 'crux' in What to Notice and the exercise is titled 'Find the rollout challenge' in student-facing text. The discipline (finding the crux) is honoured throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to M3 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close final sentence: 'The next section turns that live pattern into a small system you run on your own.' Strategy doc M3 opens with homework launch ('capable next step' mood, 'I can practice this on my own work'). The hand-off names the live pattern as the seed for personal homework — correct arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room — no authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with your neighbor,' 'share with the room,' 'turn to the person next to you,' or equivalent cohort-management sentences in student-facing body. What to Notice names the work pattern, not a room-management move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The interview forcing function ('ask one question at a time'), the push-back mechanic, and the synthesis-before-cross-pollination sequence are all encoded inside fenced prompts in find-the-crux.md. Module body prose names the ordering pattern post-hoc in What to Notice, which is appropriate post-exercise reflection, not a pre-exercise forcing function in the wrong layer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No lecture section in this module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn bullets — capability register, strategic theme verbs lead.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 11-14: 'Articulate,' 'Synthesize,' 'Evaluate,' 'Recognise.' 'Articulate' and 'Recognise' are called out explicitly in §7 as L&D facilitation register ('Articulate one key learning') and architect register ('Recognise the pairing as a repeatable design pattern') respectively. 'Recognise the structure the room produced together: individual divergence, group convergence, then cross-pollination only if it helps' has the additional apparatus tail (the colon-and-triplet definition). 'Evaluate what another group saw that your group missed, only after your own synthesis is stable' is closer to acceptable but 'only after your own synthesis is stable' is a sequencing constraint tail, not a named artifact. 'Synthesize a group's divergent rollout concerns into one named challenge' is cleanest but 'one named challenge' vs 'one named crux' slightly drifts from vocabulary the module itself uses.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace 'Articulate' with a concrete engineer-register verb (Name, Surface, Capture); replace 'Recognise the structure...' with a verb that names the action (e.g., 'Read back the ordering — diverge, synthesize, then compare — as a reusable pattern'); trim apparatus tails.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 mood is expanded horizon, not unease. Rule fires on unease modules.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names a positive discipline ('finding the crux / challenge'). No failure-mode framing in the opening. What to Notice opens with Rumelt's positive framing ('the one thing that, if you get it right, makes the rest follow').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Leap test belongs in the exercise file (find-the-crux.md), not the module wrapper. Module wrapper does not carry a leap test. N/A at module scope.
